parent.name: Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster

km.Leinster, Ireland: ឡាីនសតេរ, អៀរឡង់

zh.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh是Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster的兒子，於大約1197年出生在愛爾蘭萊音斯特。他至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh是Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster的儿子，于大约1197年出生在爱尔兰萊音斯特。他至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Kavanagh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Aileen, Brendan, Cathal, Conor, Eamon, Keelin, Liam, Michael Sean, Niall, Ronan, Sean Patrick.

Irish: from Caomhánach, an adjectival epithet meaning ‘belonging to (Saint) Caomhán’, whose personal name means ‘little beautiful one’. Etymologically, the first part of the name is the same as that in Kevin .

History: The first known bearer of the epithet was Domhnall Caomhánach (Donal Kavanagh, died in 1175), the son of Diarmait Mac Murchadha (Dermot McMurrough), king of Leinster in the 12th century. Despite being killed at an early age, Domhnall Caomhánach gave his name to a large and important family in Irish history.

variants.name: Kinsella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ciaran, Donal, Finbar, Fintan.

Irish (Wexford and Carlow): Anglicized form of Gaelic Cinnsealach, or occasionally Ó Cinnsealaigh ‘descendant of Cinnsealach’, an adjectival personal name meaning ‘proud, masterful’ or ‘chief warrior’. Cinnsealach can also indicate a member of the older population grouping called Uí Chinnsealaigh; compare the origin of Kavanagh .

variants.name: Kevin
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Caoimhín ‘descendant of Caoimhín’ (the personal name Kevin), a name derived from Caémgein ‘comely birth’ although now regarded as a diminutive of Gaelic caomh ‘comely, beloved’. Compare Kavanagh .

variants.name: Cavanaugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brennan, Aileen, Dermod, Donal, Patrick Sean.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

variants.name: Cavanah
meanings: with stress on the first syllable, this is a shortened Anglicized form of Mac Caomhánach, see Kavanagh .

alternatively, with stress on -van-, this is a shortened form of McCavana, from Mac an Mhanaigh, ‘son of the monk’ (from manach ‘monk’). This was a Mayo surname, also Anglicized by translation as Monk and Monks .
Irish:

variants.name: Cavender
meanings: English: most probably a nickname from an Anglo-Norman French equivalent of French chavender ‘chub’ (the fish).
English (of Irish origin): Anglicized form of Kavanagh (and its variants, including Cavenagh) or Cavanna . Compare Cavinder .

History: Hugh Cavenagh (died c. 1709) came from Bristol in England to Westmoreland County, VA, in 1658 as an indentured servant for a five-year period to a Mr. Wills. His descendants spell their name mainly Cavender and Cavinder.

variants.name: Kaveney
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Géibheannaigh or Mac Géibheannaigh, ‘descendant or son of Géibheannach’, a personal name based on géibheannach meaning ‘bound, or captive one’. There may have been some confusion between this name and Kavanagh .

variants.name: Cavanagh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brennan, Fergus.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

variants.name: Kavanaugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Oonagh.
Irish: variant of   Kavanagh  .

nameMeanings.name: Mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Mabel .   See also  Maeve .

nameMeanings.name: Morough
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (northern): variant of   Darragh  .

variants.name: Darragh
meanings: popularly associated with the Scottish Gaelic vocabulary word darach ‘oak’ (Irish dair, genitive darach). As an Irish name it also functions as an Anglicized form of Dáire . It is one of the Celtic names that is now spreading into more general use.

nameMeanings.name: Donell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: coined as a feminine equivalent of   Donald .

nameMeanings.name: Oge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Danish vernacular form of Aghi, a short form of the various names of Old Norse origin containing the element ag ‘edge (of a weapon)’ or ‘awe, terror’.

en.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh was born about 1197, in Leinster, Ireland as the son of Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. He had at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh est né environ 1197, à Leinster, Irlande, fils de Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. Il a eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh wurde um 1197 in Leinster, Irland, als Sohn von Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh è nato il circa 1197 nel Leinster, Irlanda come figlio di Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Leinster, Ireland: アイルランドレインスター
ja.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanaghは約1197年にアイルランドレインスターでDomhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinsterの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh(은)는 대략 1197년 아일랜드 레인스터에서 Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh nasceu aproximadamente 1197, em Leinster, Irlanda filho de Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh родился около 1197 г. в местности линстир, Ирландия. Его отца звали Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mac Morough Donell Oge Kavanagh nació aproximadamente 1197, en Leinster, Irlanda como hijo de Domhnall Caomhánach Mac Murchada, King of Leinster. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija.
